Gate boundary itself correct (401/302/login/cookie/WS-gate all as expected) BUT the child backend binds *:PORT вҖ” reachable from the LAN with no password, bypassing the gateway entirely. Spec says supervisor+child stay on 127.0.0.1.

Post-fix (child serve() now binds 127.0.0.1): full boundary as specced вҖ” 401/302/login-error/httpOnly+Secure per-port cookie/200s with cookie/forged 401/WS destroyed without cookie + 101 with; only the public port is non-loopback, child refused from LAN.

board 434563вҶ’126270 (29%), dist JS 898183вҶ’262749 (29%), both Content-Encoding: gzip only when accepted; SSE stream carries NO content-encoding and the triggered board-changed arrived ~5.4s after touch = debounce + a 2.4s board rebuild on this loaded box (build-time over budget, not a zlib stall).

Loud OPEN warning at startup + gateway logs 'OPEN (no password)'; unauth GET / вҶ’ 200 dashboard (not 302), /api/board вҶ’ 200 JSON, login layer absent (/login falls through to the dashboard, no card, no cookie check). Run with SPEXCODE_PASSWORD unset вҖ” an inherited env password correctly gates (env is a designed source).
